channel.title: Blueprints and Blueprint Books on Factorio Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Blueprints and Blueprint Books on Factorio Guide

item.title: Circuit Network Beginner Guide
item.description:
Your refineries are stuck on heavy oil again. The belts backed up last night and your entire base stalled because petroleum backed up — and nobody noticed for three hours. The fix isn't more storage tanks. It's a single red wire, a decider combinator, and 30 seconds of configuration.

TL;DR: Connect a storage tank to a pump with red wire. Decider combinator: petroleum > 20000 → output P = 1. Connect to pump. Done — automated cracking solves 90% of your fluid problems.

The Root Cause — Why Fluids Stall Without Circuits

Refineries produce heavy oil, light oil, and petroleum simultaneously. If any of the three outputs fills up, the refinery stops all production. No petroleum means no plastic. No plastic means no red circuits. No red circuits means your entire factory crawls to a halt.

item.title: How to Use Blueprints — Complete Guide
item.description:
What Are Blueprints?

Blueprints let you copy a factory design, save it for later, and paste it anywhere. They are essential for scaling your base.

Basic Blueprint Workflow

1. Copy a Design

  1. Press G (blueprint tool)
  2. Drag a rectangle around the area you want to copy
  3. The blueprint appears in your cursor — place it in your inventory

2. Save to a Blueprint Book

  1. Open your inventory
  2. Click the blueprint
  3. Click Save right arrow choose a Blueprint Book
  4. Give it a descriptive name
Naming convention: Use format [Category] Description — e.g., [Smelting] Iron 12x Furnace

3. Import a Blueprint String

  1. Copy a blueprint string (from the web or a friend)
  2. Press Ctrl+V while the blueprint tool (G) is selected
  3. The blueprint appears in your inventory

4. Export a Blueprint String

  1. Select a blueprint in your inventory
  2. Click the export button (top-right)
  3. Ctrl+C to copy the string
  4. Share it!

Blueprint Books Organization

A well-organized blueprint book saves hours of searching:
